source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Figure shows a single-gap spark chamber. The gap between the two aluminum plates of the spark chamber is $d$. A charged cosmic ray particle ionizes neon molecules along its path. After time $t$, a high-voltage pulse is applied across the two aluminum plates, and sparks are observed along the particle's path. In order to eliminate residual electrons and ions in the spark chamber from cosmic rays that entered previously, a DC voltage is applied across the two aluminum plates. This "clearing voltage" similarly sweeps away the electrons and ions we wish to observe. When the pulse is applied, what voltage $V_D$ will completely clear the space of electrons?
